recommendation: Remove the hardcoded CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID from the script and inject it via environment variables. Rotate the credential in the Cloudflare dashboard if applicable (or treat it as compromised if it were an API token). Run `git filter-repo --replace-text <(echo '1ec5420a78a852fd25bc2dd915740a71==>REDACTED')` to remove it from Git history.
No rotation necessary as Clerk publishable keys are public by design. However, remove the hardcoded value and load it from environment variables or meta tags.

Delete the ad-hoc test script `test_fix.js` from the repository. Add `test_fix.js` to `.gitignore`. Purge the file from history using `git filter-repo --invert-paths --path test_fix.js`.
